This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a comprehensive UI/UX audit of IPTVnator across all three modules (M3U, Xtream, Stalker) in both dark and light themes. They wanted to check consistency of list layouts, grid patterns, shared CSS for states/colors, and overall usability/design quality. They asked to use the frontend-design skill and agent-browser CLI to navigate and screenshot the running Electron app (CDP port 9222). After the audit plan was approved, the user asked to implement all fixes. After rebuilding, the user identified THREE additional light theme issues: (1) rail and header have different background colors, (2) visible border-right on the rail, and (3) a border-radius artifact in the top-left corner of the main content area where the background behind the rounded corner doesn't match.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Angular Material M3 theming with `$light-theme` and `$dark-theme` via `mat.define-theme()`
   - Custom CSS design tokens (`--app-*` variables) for both themes
   - Shared SCSS mixins (`_content-grid.scss`, `_portal-layout.scss`, `_portal-sidebar.scss`)
   - Workspace shell grid layout with rail, header, body, and content areas
   - CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) debugging via `agent-browser --cdp 9222`
   - Signal-based Angular components with `signal()`, `computed()`, `effect()`
   - Nx monorepo with shared libs (`libs/ui/styles/`, `libs/portal/shared/ui/`, etc.)
   - `color-mix()` CSS function for theme-aware color blending
   - `::ng-deep` deprecation and removal

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`apps/web/src/m3-theme.scss`** — Master theme file defining light/dark CSS variables
     - Changed `--app-rail-bg` from `#e8eaed` to `#f0f2f5` for better light theme icon contrast
     - Key light theme tokens: `--app-shell-bg: #eef0f3`, `--app-rail-bg: #f0f2f5`, `--

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Continue UI/UX audit fixes for IPTVnator across M3U, Xtream, and Stalker modules in dark and light themes
   - Fix three light theme issues: rail/header bg mismatch, rail border-right, corner border-radius artifact
   - Fix settings icon not visible at bottom of rail
   - Remove rail border-right in dark theme too (after recommendation)
   - Audit playlist-switcher component for consistency and fix issues
   - Fix disappeared hover effects in rail navigation and playlist-switcher
   - Audit EPG progress panel style and UX for both themes
   - Brainstorm centralized notification panel idea (skeptically, using plan mode)
   - Simplify and align EPG panel style with app design language after initial fixes felt too heavy

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Angular Material M3 theming with `$light-theme` and `$dark-theme` via `mat.define-theme()`
   - **Critical discovery: `--mat-sys-*` tokens are NOT available as global CSS custom properties** — they only exist within Angular Material component scopes. Any `color-mix()` or direct use of `--mat-sys-on-surface`, `--mat-sys-surface-container` etc. outside Material components resolves to transparent/empty.
   - Custom `--app-*` CSS design tokens defined in `m3-theme.scss` (light on `html {}`, dark under `.dark-theme {}`)
   - Theme switching via `document.body.classList.add/remove('dark-theme')` in SettingsService
   - `box-sizing: border-box` fix for CSS grid overflow with padding
   - `color-mix(in srgb, ...)` CSS function for theme-aware opacity overlays
   - Signal-based Angular components with `signal()`, `computed()`
   - CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) debugging via `agent-browser --cdp 9222`
   - EPG panel `:host-context(.light-theme)` never matches because app uses `dark-theme` class (not `light-theme`)

3. Files and Code Se
